title: GET a URL and write the HTTP status code
prompt: Read a URL from standard input (a single line, trailing newline allowed). Issue an HTTP GET request to that URL with a 5000 ms transport timeout. Write the HTTP response status code as decimal followed by a newline to standard output, e.g. `200\n`. If the transport fails for any reason (DNS, connect, TLS, timeout, invalid URL, unsupported protocol, provider unavailable, I/O), write the literal string `error\n` to standard output. Do not write to standard error. Exit with status 0 in every case.

input_schema.stdin: <one URL, up to 256 bytes including optional trailing newline>

output_schema.stdout: either `<decimal_status>\n` on transport success or `error\n` on any transport failure

notes_input_convention: Zero reads the URL from argv[1] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability; the URL is interpreted identically to a stdin read in the other languages (trailing newline trimmed if present). All other languages read the URL from stdin until newline or EOF. The verifier spins up a local HTTP fixture server before each language run and tears it down after; tests reference its base URL. The verifier passes the URL in stdin for non-Zero refs and in argv[1] for Zero.
